stories.headline: Senate impeachment court elects Escudero presiding officer, sets 16-vote bar to convict Duterte
stories.summary: The Senate impeachment court opened Vice President Sara Duterte's trial on July 6, electing Senator Francis Escudero presiding officer on a 12-8 vote and affirming that 16 of the 24 senator-judges must vote to convict. It issued subpoenas for the bank records of Duterte and her husband Manases Carpio, summoned two National Bureau of Investigation agents, ordered a sealed box of tax records returned to the Bureau of Internal Revenue for lack of custody authority, and heard opening statements. Duterte did not attend, her camp citing her right against self-incrimination.
stories.context: Duterte was impeached by the House for a second time on May 11, 2026, on four articles; the Senate has allotted 92 trial days, split between prosecution and defense. The minority bloc objected that the Constitution requires the Senate President to preside.
stories.watch_for: The trial resumes July 7, when House prosecutors are expected to call their first witnesses.

stories.headline: Marcos wraps up Canada visit with about $2.5 billion in investment pledges
stories.summary: President Marcos concluded a three-day official visit to Canada with about $2.5 billion in investment pledges from Canadian firms including B2Gold, OceanaGold, TELUS and NQX, spanning mining, critical minerals, energy and IT-BPM, and said the two countries would pursue a proposed ASEAN-Canada free trade agreement. The Department of Trade and Industry separately said it is in early talks with Canadian firm AtkinsRealis on nuclear-energy cooperation.
stories.context: The visit follows the Philippines' recent World Bank reclassification as an upper-middle-income economy and was described by officials as the first official visit by a Philippine president to Canada in over a decade.

stories.headline: Marcos denies role in flood-control scandal, says corruption probe 'not done yet'
stories.summary: President Marcos denied involvement in the flood-control infrastructure corruption scandal and rejected calls for his resignation over the pace of the investigation, telling reporters in Vancouver that the probe is not finished and that findings increasingly point to wrongdoing predating his administration. He said his government was the first to expose and act on the irregularities.
stories.context: Marcos created the Independent Commission for Infrastructure in September 2025 to investigate anomalous flood-control spending; the scandal has drawn street protests and opposition demands for accountability.

stories.headline: Marcos calls China's sanctions on Defense Secretary Teodoro 'unhelpful'
stories.summary: President Marcos described China's decision to sanction Defense Secretary Gilberto Teodoro Jr. as 'very unhelpful,' saying it raises tensions and complicates defense communication rather than easing the South China Sea dispute. China sanctioned Teodoro and his family on June 11, barring them from mainland China, Hong Kong and Macau, saying he made 'irresponsible remarks.'
stories.context: The sanctions come amid continued friction over the West Philippine Sea, an issue Marcos has called central to his administration's foreign policy.

stories.headline: Senator Marcoleta arrested on plunder charge over alleged undeclared P75-million donations
stories.summary: Senator Rodante Marcoleta was arrested on July 6 after the Sandiganbayan's Third Division denied his motion to quash and issued arrest warrants in a plunder case over an alleged undeclared P75 million in 2025 campaign contributions. Former party-list representative Michael Defensor and two businessmen named as co-accused were also arrested. The accused were booked by the Criminal Investigation and Detection Group and taken to a Quezon City jail. Marcoleta has denied wrongdoing, and plunder is a non-bailable offense.
stories.context: The Ombudsman filed plunder and bribery charges against Marcoleta and three businessmen on July 3 over the alleged undeclared 2025 campaign contributions. He remains a sitting senator-judge in the concurrent impeachment trial of Vice President Sara Duterte.

stories.headline: Marcos says lowering the age of criminal liability needs further study
stories.summary: President Marcos said proposals to lower the minimum age of criminal liability from 15 to 12, raised after recent school-violence incidents, require further expert study, particularly on how social media and violent video games affect youth behavior. He cited a prior government restriction on the video game 'GoreBox' after a shooting suspect was linked to it.

stories.headline: DPWH keeps district engineers' authority to bid and award contracts up to P150 million
stories.summary: Public Works Secretary Vince Dizon issued a July 1 department order retaining the authority of DPWH district engineers and regional directors to certify funds and sign infrastructure contracts worth up to P150 million, keeping the threshold set by his predecessor. This is despite an earlier recommendation from the now-defunct Independent Commission for Infrastructure to lower the threshold following the flood-control corruption scandal.

stories.headline: Philippine stocks post fourth straight gain ahead of June inflation data; peso slips
stories.summary: The Philippine Stock Exchange index rose 0.58%, or 35.96 points, to close at 6,223.99 on July 6, its fourth straight advance, as investors positioned for June inflation data expected to show a further slowdown. The peso weakened to P61.491 per dollar from P61.415 on Friday. Analysts polled by local outlets estimated June inflation at roughly 6.5% to 6.6%, versus 6.8% in May.
stories.watch_for: The Philippine Statistics Authority is due to release the June Consumer Price Index on July 7.

stories.headline: Quezon City reports COVID-19 uptick; mayor says cases mild and manageable
stories.summary: Quezon City Mayor Joy Belmonte said a recent rise in COVID-19 cases in the city is mild and manageable, with cases isolated and no clustering detected, after the city's epidemiology division reported 146 cases so far in 2026 — a 265% increase over three weeks, from 23 cases in May 21-June 10 to 84 in June 11-July 1. Children 14 and under accounted for nearly 30% of cases. The city said 66 health centers offer free consultation, testing and medicine.

stories.headline: Marcos orders storm preparations as Super Typhoon Bavi nears Philippines after hitting Marianas
stories.summary: President Marcos ordered a whole-of-government preparedness effort on July 6 as Super Typhoon Bavi, to be locally named Inday, tracked toward the Philippines from the western Pacific. PAGASA said Bavi remained a super typhoon thousands of kilometers east of the country and was expected to enter the Philippine Area of Responsibility around July 8. Al Jazeera reported the storm made landfall earlier on Rota in the Northern Mariana Islands, a US territory, causing major damage and power outages. A direct hit on the Philippine mainland is still considered unlikely.
stories.context: Bavi is the ninth tropical cyclone to affect the Philippines in 2026. Forecasters have said it may pass near Batanes en route to Taiwan and could enhance the southwest monsoon rather than make direct landfall on the mainland.
stories.watch_for: PAGASA said the enhanced southwest monsoon could bring heavy rain to parts of Luzon and the Visayas around July 8-10, and wind signals may be raised over northern Luzon as the storm approaches.

stories.headline: Researchers identify new fault zones off Luzon, raising earthquake and tsunami risk
stories.summary: A joint Taiwanese-Filipino research team identified four previously unknown fault zones branching from the Philippine Fault Zone in waters west of Luzon, with one branch appearing linked to the Manila Trench, according to findings published in the Journal of Asian Earth Sciences. The researchers said the discovery points to elevated risks of major earthquakes and tsunami generation in the area and should inform hazard planning.

stories.headline: Water crisis worsens in Real, Quezon province as drought forces daily rationing
stories.summary: Residents of Real, Quezon province are facing a worsening water shortage after drought significantly reduced local river flows, prompting local authorities to impose daily water rationing.

stories.headline: Mayon Volcano's effusive eruption continues for 183rd day, Alert Level 3 remains
stories.summary: PHIVOLCS said Mayon Volcano's effusive eruption continued for a 183rd consecutive day, with lava flows, pyroclastic density currents and rockfalls recorded overnight. Alert Level 3 remains in effect, and entry into the 6-kilometer permanent danger zone remains prohibited.

stories.headline: Alex Eala upsets Iga Swiatek to reach Wimbledon fourth round
stories.summary: Filipino tennis player Alex Eala defeated defending champion Iga Swiatek 7-6(9), 6-2 to reach the Wimbledon round of 16, where she is set to face Italy's Jasmine Paolini for a place in the quarterfinals.
stories.context: A quarterfinal berth would make Eala the first Filipino to reach a Grand Slam quarterfinal in decades, according to Inquirer.
stories.watch_for: Eala faces Paolini in the round of 16.

stories.headline: Justin Brownlee ruled out of Gilas Pilipinas game against Australia
stories.summary: The Samahang Basketbol ng Pilipinas said naturalized forward Justin Brownlee will miss Gilas Pilipinas's FIBA World Cup Asian qualifying game against Australia due to a hamstring strain and other leg injuries, following a double-overtime loss to New Zealand days earlier.

stories.headline: Iran holds main Tehran procession for slain Supreme Leader Khamenei
stories.summary: Iran held the main funeral procession through Tehran on July 6 for assassinated Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei, following two days of lying-in-state, with authorities anticipating vast crowds. Mourners carried the flag-draped coffins of Khamenei and four family members killed in a February 28 airstrike on his office, along with portraits of his son Mojtaba Khamenei, whom Al Jazeera described as Iran's new supreme leader. The procession is set to move on to Qom and then to Najaf and Karbala in Iraq before burial in Mashhad.
stories.context: Khamenei was killed alongside family members in the opening strikes of the US-Israeli war on Iran in February 2026; the funeral comes amid a fragile US-Iran ceasefire.
stories.watch_for: The procession is expected to continue to Qom on July 7, then to Najaf and Karbala in Iraq on July 8, with burial in Mashhad on July 9.

stories.headline: Trump to meet Zelenskyy and Syria's al-Sharaa at NATO summit amid Ukraine battlefield dispute
stories.summary: The White House said President Trump will meet Turkish President Erdogan on July 7 and separately meet Ukraine's President Zelenskyy and Syria's Ahmad al-Sharaa on July 8 during a NATO summit in Ankara, with Zelenskyy citing 'a real prospect' of ending the war with Russia. The announcement came after Russia's military said it had captured the eastern Ukrainian city of Kostiantynivka, a claim Ukraine's General Staff and Zelenskyy denied as false; Reuters said it could not independently verify either side's battlefield claims.
stories.context: The exchange follows a Trump-Putin phone call the Kremlin described as 'businesslike and constructive,' in which Trump reportedly offered to help end the war and floated sending envoys to Moscow.
stories.watch_for: NATO summit in Ankara July 7-8, including planned Trump meetings with Erdogan, Zelenskyy and al-Sharaa.

stories.headline: Detained Gaza hospital director in life-threatening condition, rights group says
stories.summary: Physicians for Human Rights Israel and family members said Dr. Hussam Abu Safia, director of Gaza's Kamal Adwan Hospital, is in life-threatening condition after more than 555 days in Israeli detention without charge. His lawyer, who visited him on July 2, said he documented fresh bruising, breathing difficulty and loss of consciousness following Abu Safia's transfer to a prison's solitary-confinement section.
stories.context: Abu Safia was detained by Israeli forces in late 2024; rights groups have repeatedly raised concerns about his treatment and health in custody.

stories.headline: Deadly clashes between Houthi and government forces reported in Yemen's Hodeidah
stories.summary: Houthi forces killed 16 pro-government Yemeni troops and wounded 22 others in the Jabal Dabbas area of Hodeidah governorate overnight, according to a Yemeni military official cited by Al Jazeera, in fighting described as among the deadliest in years. Houthi forces briefly seized positions before a government counterattack retook them by dawn; Yemeni officials said more than 50 Houthi fighters were killed, though this was not independently confirmed.

stories.headline: Russian missile and drone barrage on Kyiv kills at least 11 on eve of NATO summit
stories.summary: At least 11 people were killed and more than 50 wounded in an overnight Russian missile and drone attack on Kyiv early on July 6, according to Ukrainian authorities, who said Russia fired 68 missiles and 351 drones in waves before dawn. Officials said residential buildings in several districts, including the historic Podil area, were damaged. President Zelenskyy, who had warned of an imminent strike, said the timing came just before a NATO summit in Turkey where he is due to meet US President Trump.
stories.context: The attack was among the largest on the Ukrainian capital in recent weeks and came as Russia and Ukraine traded disputed claims over control of the eastern city of Kostiantynivka.
stories.watch_for: A NATO summit opens July 7 in Ankara, where Trump is expected to hold separate meetings with the Turkish, Ukrainian and Syrian leaders.
